question:
A lottery offers a grand prize of $\$ 10$ million. The probability of winning this grand prize is 1 in 55 million $\left(\approx 1.8 	imes 10^{-8}ight)$. There are no other prizes, so the probability of winning nothing $=1-(1.8 	imes \left.10^8ight)=0.999999982$. Table 5.5 shows the probability mass function for the problem.

(a) What is the expected value of a lottery ticket?
(b) Fifty-five million lottery tickets will be sold. How much does the proprietor of the lottery need to charge per ticket to make a profit?
